steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim states that Camilla experiences a change in how she perceives her immediate surroundings. The correct quotation must show her surroundings staying the same physically while her perception of them shifts — not a change in her actions, emotions, or self-reflection.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — “Camilla stared up at the blue sky so [Luis] should not read the expression in her eyes.”: Camilla is concealing her expression from another character. There is no description of surroundings, and nothing changes in how she perceives them. ✗
  • Choice B — “Camilla, standing on the beach of washed, round stones, caught a reflection of herself in the waters.”: Camilla sees herself reflected in water — self-reflection, not a changed perception of the environment around her. ✗
  • Choice C — “All nature was as she had found it before, evening upon evening; but in the stones and the dry weeds, amid the fragrance of the air and the light whir of falling leaves, Camilla sensed a new strangeness, a vast desolation in everything about her.”: The surroundings are explicitly unchanged (“all nature was as she had found it before”), yet Camilla now perceives them as strange and desolate. This is exactly the shift in perception the claim describes. ✓
  • Choice D — “[Camilla] closed her eyes fast to hold back the tears that welled up in them. Then, with the back of her hand, she wiped her wet cheeks, and just as she had done three days ago, fled with all the swiftness of a young deer.”: Shows emotional distress and physical flight. Her surroundings are not described, and no perceptual shift is depicted. ✗
C is correct.
